channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: CSN loses $7.4M in 'Ghost Students' scheme
item.description: Cyber hackers targeted the College of Southern Nevada, and the school is now on the hook for millions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he sent a letter to Iran's supreme leader over country's advancing nuclear program
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump sent a letter to Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, seeking a new deal with Tehran to restrain its rapidly advancing nuclear program and replace the agreement he withdrew America from in his first term in office.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bragg to Liberty and back again: Ceremony to rechristen Army post once named for a Confederate
item.description: FORT BRAGG, N.C. (AP) — The short-lived existence of Fort Liberty is set to an end Friday when the nation’s largest Army installation officially returns to its former name: Fort Bragg. Christened a century ago in honor of Confederate Gen.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN BREED and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order creating bitcoin reserve
item.description: Trump, who once called crypto a "scam," has drastically changed his tune on the subject and promised on the campaign trail to make the U.S. the "crypto capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Officials to give update on Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa death investigation: What to know
item.description: The Santa Fe County Sheriff’s Office is planning to share an update on its investigation into the mysterious deaths of Gene Hackman and wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KREX 5

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — From heartbreak and devastation to outrage, Emily Pike's tragic death is stirring heavy emotions and spotlighting a crisis that has long plagued Native American communities, where a disproportionate number of people have been killed or have gone missing.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Homeland Security ends collective bargaining agreement with TSA staffers, an attack on worker rights
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major attack on worker rights under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Broadway's 'Wicked' welcomes Lencia Kebede, its first pioneering full-time Black Elphaba
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Green tears were flowing when Lencia Kebede made history this week on Broadway, becoming the first Black actor to assume the role of Elphaba full time in the Broadway company of “Wicked.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 12 indicted migrants allegedly assaulted officers at South Texas ICE lockup
item.description: A federal grand jury in South Texas has indicted 10 Mexican nationals and two men from Guatemala on charges of assaulting two correctional officers, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: 2 more arrested in theft of bulldog puppies from Colorado store
item.description: Deputies arrested the man who allegedly snatched a pair of bulldog puppies from an enclosure at a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas City Chiefs fans’ cause of death revealed after being found frozen in friend’s yard
item.description: The three Kansas City Chiefs fans found frozen in their friend's backyard died of fentanyl and cocaine toxicity, as new details about their bizarre deaths unravel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KRON4

item.title: Flight attendant pleads guilty to secretly recording a 14-year-old girl in plane bathroom
item.description: A former American Airlines flight attendant has pleaded guilty to secretly recording video of a 14-year-old girl using an airplane bathroom.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: List of Social Security Administration offices DOGE is closing
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency has started to make large budget cuts, resulting in the closure of multiple SSA offices.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge deciding whether to let charges drop against NYC Mayor Eric Adams is about to get key advice
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy Amphibious Base Coronado in San Diego County has been the subject of controversy for decades due to the exterior design of four barracks that resemble a swastika.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Fire leads police to body found in clothing donation bin
item.description: Police are investigating a body found in a clothing donation bin in the parking lot of 70 Hudson Avenue. According to the Glens Falls Police Department, around 2:21 a.m., officers responded to an active fire at the donation bin and discovered the deceased person inside.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Abel, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As DOGE slashes spending, it's missing millions of dollars hidden in plain sight
item.description: As DOGE looks for efficiencies, it may need to start with the government’s own money sitting unclaimed in many states, held by the government.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Ohio State Highway Patrol supervisors punished after reported abuse
item.description: What began as an investigation into one man resulted in repercussions for two additional men in positions of power as the investigative process progressed.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Stone, NewsNation

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida sheriff says no active shooter at Navy installation after massive police response
item.description: Escambia County Sheriff Chip Simmons said that there was no sign of an active shooter at Corry Station on March 6. In 2019, the base experienced a terror attack that killed three sailors and wounded eight others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Job cuts last month higher than any February since 2009
item.description: U.S.-based employers cut more jobs last month than in any February in 16 years, according to a new report from outplacement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs, according to internal memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning a reorganization that includes cutting over 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care and other services for millions of veterans, according to an internal memo obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Trump announces 1-month delay on Canada, Mexico tariffs for cars
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced a one-month exemption on tariffs against Canada and Mexico for cars following a meeting with the three major U.S. automakers.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Joann store closures: See the list of nearly 800 locations headed for sale
item.description: After more than 80 years, national fabric and craft retailer Joann is another step closer to being erased from shopping centers across the U.S. after the release of nearly 800 locations going up for sale in the coming months.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5
